title: Ming Foundation Current RFC Requirement Registry

authority.normative_source: The RFC Markdown documents in YuemingHub/Ming-Foundation
authority.registry_role: Current derived machine-readable index; never overrides source text

requirements.source_marker: An assertion MUST be treated as material when at least one of these applies:
requirements.statement: An assertion is classified as material when any persistence, sharing, decision, identity, downstream-use, or difficult-reversal criterion applies.

requirements.source_marker: When materiality is uncertain, the assertion MUST be treated as material
requirements.statement: Uncertain materiality defaults to material until a role-appropriate review records a narrower classification.

requirements.source_marker: A use is high impact when it can:
requirements.statement: A use is classified as high impact when it can cause irreversible, punitive, rights-restricting, sensitive-disclosure, institutional, or durable-profile consequences.

requirements.source_marker: A high-impact classification MUST record the responsible actor
requirements.statement: Every high-impact classification records responsible actor, basis, affected people, consequence, duration, review point, and appeal or correction route.
Representative authority records type, subject, evidence, allowed and prohibited actions, validity, conflicts, notice, and independent-review route.

requirements.source_marker: Age alone MUST NOT establish complete representative authority.
requirements.statement: Age alone does not establish complete representative authority.

requirements.source_marker: A governed disclosure basis MUST state:
requirements.statement: A governed disclosure basis records purpose, authority, minimum necessity, recipient, duration, onward-use limits, notice or delay reason, constraints, reviewer, and appeal.
Every material assertion distinguishes subject, speaker, knowledge-object type, evidence, uncertainty, confirmation or contestation, purpose, impact, visibility, active period, and dependent records.

requirements.source_marker: No third-party statement, professional hypothesis, or model inference may
requirements.statement: A third-party statement, professional hypothesis, or model inference does not silently become a subject-confirmed fact.
A subject record contains identity, type, age or capacity band where relevant, relationship, participation state, visibility policy, representative references, jurisdiction profile, and review date.
A speaker record contains identity, type, role, relationship, authority scope, conflict status, and source time.

An assertion record contains source, status, content, evidence, confidence, uncertainty, materiality, impact, validity, purpose, visibility, contestation, dependencies, lineage, and supersession fields.
A contestation record contains requester authority, requested action, reason, constraints, conflict check, reviewer, decision, dependent-record actions, appeal, and resolution time.
Implementations represent the complete minimum knowledge-status vocabulary defined by RFC-0001.
Simplified user-interface labels preserve inspectable stored source, confidence, dispute, and revision distinctions.

requirements.source_marker: Participation MUST be determined through an age-, capacity-, context-,
requirements.statement: Participation is determined through an age-, capacity-, context-, safety-, impact-, and jurisdiction-aware profile.

requirements.source_marker: The implementation MUST NOT infer a state solely from age.
requirements.statement: Participation state is not inferred solely from age.

requirements.source_marker: use MUST pause for independent or conflict-free review
requirements.statement: A representative conflict pauses high-impact use for independent or conflict-free review unless a documented immediate safety duty requires temporary action.

requirements.source_marker: A temporary participation restriction MUST record actor, basis, scope,
requirements.statement: A temporary participation restriction records actor, basis, scope, duration, minimum disclosure, review date, and appeal or later-notice route.

requirements.source_marker: A high-impact contested assertion SHOULD NOT drive an irreversible,
requirements.statement: A high-impact contested assertion should not drive irreversible, punitive, rights-restricting, or identity-shaping action while review is pending.

requirements.source_marker: An exception requires an immediate safety or legal duty and MUST record:
requirements.statement: A safety or legal exception to the contestability pause records actor, evidence, urgency, scope, alternatives, safeguards, deadline, later notice, and appeal.

requirements.source_marker: One family member's private statement MUST NOT be exposed to another without
requirements.statement: A family member's private statement is not exposed to another person without a governed disclosure basis.
Disclosure is minimum necessary and excludes unrelated, unnecessarily verbatim, protected-source, disputed-as-fact, and expired information.

requirements.source_marker: When notice is delayed for safety, the delay MUST be specific
requirements.statement: Delayed notice for safety is specific, review-bounded, and independently reviewable.

requirements.source_marker: Imported or legacy assertions MUST retain:
requirements.statement: Imported or legacy assertions retain original source, status, visibility or basis where known, transformation history, migration actor, uncertainty, and dependencies.

requirements.source_marker: A correction or supersession MUST propagate
requirements.statement: Correction or supersession propagates to active dependent decisions, profiles, summaries, and retrieval indexes while preserving audit evidence.

requirements.source_marker: Where safe rollback is not possible, the limitation and remediation plan
requirements.statement: When safe rollback is impossible, the limitation and remediation plan are recorded.
The implementation performs any prohibited identity, inference, dispute, source-removal, contestability, disclosure, authority, or expired-use behavior listed in RFC-0001.

requirements.source_marker: A person reviewing an interpretation SHOULD be able to see:
requirements.statement: A person reviewing an interpretation can inspect source, speaker, AI involvement, evidence, uncertainty, status, impact, active period, disputes, authority basis, correction route, and dependencies.

requirements.source_marker: Every implementation MUST declare a jurisdiction profile that identifies:
requirements.statement: Every implementation declares a jurisdiction profile covering locations, asserted bases, age and representative rules, rights, retention, safeguarding duties, conflicts, owner, and review date.

requirements.source_marker: implementation MUST NOT claim that processing is lawful merely because it
requirements.statement: Absent, expired, or uncertain jurisdiction qualification does not support a universal lawfulness claim.

requirements.source_marker: Every material collection or use MUST have a traceable basis containing:
requirements.statement: Every material collection or use has a traceable basis covering subject, operator, purpose, category, jurisdiction, data, access, use, retention, review, rights, authority, and exceptions.
Future improvement, unknown future value, or mere data existence is not a sufficient purpose.

requirements.source_marker: An implementation MUST distinguish at least:
requirements.statement: Implementations distinguish optional permission, requested service, safety, mandatory, security, approved research or evaluation, and unknown or disputed basis categories.

requirements.source_marker: A jurisdiction profile MUST state whether and
requirements.statement: A jurisdiction profile states whether and how each technical category maps to a lawful or professional basis.

requirements.source_marker: Mandatory, emergency, safety, or institutional processing MUST NOT be
requirements.statement: Mandatory, emergency, safety, or institutional processing is not represented as freely given consent.
A purpose definition contains identity, description, affected people, data, operations, recipients, basis categories, jurisdiction, retention, risk, owner, and status.

A permission or processing-basis record contains person or representative, authority, purpose, category, jurisdiction, scope, version, time, review, withdrawal, constraints, exceptions, notice, and conflict status.
Representative authority records identity, subject, type, evidence, allowed and prohibited actions, conflicts, validity, notice, and independent review.

requirements.source_marker: Age or family role alone MUST NOT create unlimited authority.
requirements.statement: Age or family role alone does not create unlimited representative authority.
Conflict, disputed authority, or high-impact rights requests route to a conflict-free reviewer.
A data asset inventory contains subjects, source, category, sensitivity, locations, purposes, roles, retention, deletion dependencies, backup, derivation, export, and audit references.
Rights-request support includes access, explanation, correction, contextualization, restriction, objection, export, deletion, withdrawal, high-impact review, and appeal.

A rights-request record contains requester, represented subject, authority evidence, conflict check, scope, verification, owner, status, located data, derived and backup locations, exceptions, decision, evidence, independent review, and appeal.

requirements.source_marker: Every asset MUST map to a named retention profile.
requirements.statement: Every asset maps to a named retention profile.
A retention profile records purpose, covered data, locations, access, start, review, disposition trigger, backup handling, exceptions, owner, maximum exception or review, evidence, and appeal.
Implementations distinguish transient intake, active service, safety or incident, security and audit, restricted validation, and public-record retention profiles.
Each retention duration is defined and justified in the applicable jurisdiction and service profile.

requirements.source_marker: Indefinite retention for unspecified improvement MUST NOT be permitted.
requirements.statement: Data is not retained indefinitely for unspecified improvement.
Material data processing follows a traceable lifecycle from purpose and review through collection, use, periodic review, rights action, propagation, completion or exception, evidence, and appeal.

requirements.source_marker: It MUST maintain an explicit authority map
requirements.statement: A centralized, federated, or hybrid implementation maintains an explicit authority map for identity, purpose, permission, correction, representative authority, retention, and audit.

requirements.source_marker: a verified subject correction supersedes an inference or third-party
requirements.statement: A verified subject correction supersedes inference or third-party assertion as the current interpretation without deleting history.

requirements.source_marker: the most restrictive current instruction governs optional use
requirements.statement: The most restrictive current instruction governs optional use while an instruction conflict is reviewed.

requirements.source_marker: an unresolved identity or authority conflict freezes expansion, sharing,
requirements.statement: An unresolved identity or authority conflict freezes expansion, sharing, training, and high-impact use.

requirements.source_marker: safety or mandatory exceptions require a specific basis
requirements.statement: Safety or mandatory exceptions record a specific basis, minimum scope, named actor, and review point.

requirements.source_marker: correction, withdrawal, and deletion events MUST propagate
requirements.statement: Correction, withdrawal, and deletion propagate across linked channels and derived assets.

requirements.source_marker: a channel-specific record MUST NOT become an untraceable shadow profile.
requirements.statement: A channel-specific record does not become an untraceable shadow profile.
Imported data preserves source context, original purpose and basis where known, transformations, authority, derivations, dependencies, uncertainty, and remediation limits.

requirements.source_marker: A rights request is not complete until active primary, derived, indexed,
requirements.statement: A rights request is not complete until primary, derived, indexed, shared, and scheduled backup handling is addressed or narrowly excepted.

requirements.source_marker: General early-access or contact forms SHOULD:
requirements.statement: General forms minimize fields, state purpose and retention, identify recipients and follow-up, and provide a rights route.

requirements.source_marker: prohibit identifiable child, medical, crisis, or private consultation
requirements.statement: General forms prohibit identifiable child, medical, crisis, or private consultation details unless separately governed secure intake exists.
General forms avoid pre-checked optional permissions and separate newsletter, research, product-access, and consultation purposes.

The implementation performs any prohibited collection, reuse, scattering, indefinite retention, hidden exception, false consent, excessive access, unlimited authority, permissive conflict, or incomplete-deletion behavior listed in RFC-0002.

requirements.source_marker: A safety action is high impact when it can
requirements.statement: A safety action is classified as high impact when it can restrict rights, disclose sensitive information, involve external authority, create durable risk labels, change service, or be difficult to reverse.

requirements.source_marker: Every high-impact safety action MUST have:
requirements.statement: Every high-impact safety action records actual authority, evidence, uncertainty, severity, bounded action, proportionality, handoff or availability, review, and correction or appeal.

requirements.source_marker: A queue, model, mailbox, or unnamed team is not a named responsible role.
requirements.statement: A queue, model, mailbox, or unnamed team is not treated as a named responsible role.

requirements.source_marker: `P0` means credible evidence indicates imminent or unfolding severe harm
requirements.statement: P0 classification means credible imminent or unfolding severe harm where delay may materially increase danger.

requirements.source_marker: `P1` means credible serious risk requires prompt accountable review
requirements.statement: P1 classification means credible serious risk requiring prompt accountable review without current evidence establishing immediate P0.
A safety classification records recency, report source, plan or means where relevant, protective factors, uncertainty, classifier, and next review.

requirements.source_marker: A classification profile MUST define response targets and escalation
requirements.statement: Each service model defines response targets and escalation ownership without creating a universal emergency-response promise.

requirements.source_marker: Every implementation MUST publish and record one current service profile:
requirements.statement: Every implementation publishes and records one current service profile: active handoff, bounded review, no handoff, or unverified.
A service profile records responsible role, hours, response target, locations, languages, unavailable services, failed-handoff action, verification, expiry, and owner.

requirements.source_marker: A `no_handoff` or `unverified` system MUST NOT mark a safety handoff complete
requirements.statement: A no-handoff or unverified service does not mark handoff complete or imply active human monitoring.
A safety-signal record contains source, subject, reporter, time, category, evidence, uncertainty, model or policy version, recency, and direct or third-party status.
A safety-assessment record contains signals, risk, immediacy, potential harm, protective factors, unknowns, recommended action, least-intrusive alternative, reviewer, time, and next review.
A handoff record contains service profile, responsible role, target, initiation, acknowledgment, acceptance, disclosure, basis, target time, state, failure action, closure reason, and authority.
Handoff states distinguish initiated, acknowledged, accepted, failed, redirected, and closed.

requirements.source_marker: A handoff MUST NOT be marked closed merely because a message was sent.
requirements.statement: A handoff is not closed merely because a message was sent.
A safety-action record contains actor, authority, action, scope, duration, affected people, notice, delay reason, proportionality, review, and appeal.
An incident review records severity, harm or near miss, false-positive or negative status, timeline, responsibility, causes, privacy impact, remediation, recurrence controls, and closure authority.

requirements.source_marker: The selected response SHOULD be the least intrusive action
requirements.statement: The selected safety response is the least intrusive action that reasonably addresses the identified risk.

requirements.source_marker: For every high-impact action, the record MUST state:
requirements.statement: Every high-impact action records harm, evidence, uncertainty, urgency, preference, alternatives, insufficiency reason, privacy cost, scope, duration, safeguards, review, and rollback or appeal.

requirements.source_marker: A more intrusive temporary action under severe uncertainty requires a
requirements.statement: A more intrusive temporary action under severe uncertainty has a precautionary basis, shortest reasonable duration, and independent or conflict-free review.

The safety path preserves classification, availability check, assessment, proportionality, temporary response, accountable handoff or redirection, state tracking, notice or appeal, incident review, and remediation.

requirements.source_marker: MingOS MUST NOT claim to be:
requirements.statement: MingOS does not claim emergency response, diagnosis, legal advice, child-protection authority, or guaranteed human response unless actually staffed and governed.

requirements.source_marker: Public and in-product messaging MUST state:
requirements.statement: Public and in-product messages state service profile, AI or human status, capabilities and limits, geography, local support route, resource owner, verification, and expiry.

requirements.source_marker: Expired or unverified local resources MUST NOT be presented as current.
requirements.statement: Expired or unverified local resources are not presented as current.
Safety review preserves evidence, model and policy version, reviewer, classification changes, false outcomes, downstream effects, propagation, and appeal history.

requirements.source_marker: A disputed, expired, or superseded safety label MUST NOT become a permanent
requirements.statement: A disputed, expired, or superseded safety label does not become a permanent identity marker or silently drive recommendations.

requirements.source_section: 9. Operator may be the risk source
requirements.source_marker: Independent or conflict-free review MUST be triggered when:
requirements.statement: Operator-as-risk, conflict, rights restriction, disputed high-impact classification, protected-information request, or self-closing authority triggers independent or conflict-free review with bounded disclosure, protection, authority, restrictions, notice, and appeal.

requirements.source_marker: Historical risk records MUST retain source, policy version, classification
requirements.statement: Historical risk records preserve source, policy, classification history, and downstream effects; corrections propagate, and incomplete rollback records exposure and remediation.
Case and cross-family evidence remains purpose-limited, access-restricted, provenance-preserving, and resistant to re-identification.
Public repository records should contain method, limitations, aggregate findings, objections, and decisions.
Public records must not contain raw identifiable family or child material.

Cross-family analysis states purpose, minimizes fields, documents inclusion and exclusion, preserves uncertainty and counterexamples, avoids ranking, restricts raw-case browsing, receives privacy and bias review, and expires or revalidates derived rules.
External model providers must not receive case data merely because the application uses their API.
Raw family cases must not be committed to the public repository.
Recognizable combinations must not be published as anonymous cases.
Consultation data must not be reused for marketing.
Cross-family analysis must not create family scores or rankings.
Rare cases must not be used as model examples without separate review.
Analysts must not access full cases when aggregates suffice.
Generated summaries must not be treated as de-identified by default.
Data must not be preserved indefinitely because future research may be useful.
Every material public claim is traceable to a repository source, evidence source, or explicit capability-status label.
Official public surfaces should use the defined capability-status vocabulary.
Vision must not be worded as a delivered capability.
A public Charter summary states that it is a summary, identifies the governing document, status, version, synchronization date, change history, and discrepancy-report route.
The website must not silently rewrite Candidate or Accepted commitments for marketing.
Material architecture, Charter, product, and governance pages should display source ID, version or commit, status, review date, capability status where relevant, owner, and correction route.
A material claim record should preserve exact wording, audience, supporting source, limitations, reviewer, publication date, and expiry or re-review date.
Public forms state purpose, required and optional fields, recipient, retention, request route, human or automated follow-up, prohibited sensitive content, and relevant privacy notice.
A general early-access form should not solicit identifiable child, crisis, medical, or consultation details.
Official surfaces make visible AI involvement, fallibility, human-review status, available professional role, unavailable emergency or clinical services, and response-availability limits.
A future implementation should provide repository-to-site synchronization, a normative-page manifest, status/version checks, stale-content release controls, discrepancy reporting, and release notes.
